summary: Use the persisted `examples/lead-app-ts` app as the source of truth. For this slice, one TS-only benchmark plus one explicit incompatible-schema regression was enough.
ergonomics: Task discovery needed no harness changes because `run-benchmark.mjs` already discovers manifests from `benchmarks/tasks/*/task.json`.
`benchmarks/test-task-prep.sh` is the key guardrail here; it proves both that the new repo starts incomplete and that copying the canonical persisted app files is sufficient to reach green.
The repeated Nix dirty-tree warnings add a lot of noise during benchmark verification but did not affect outcomes.
follow_ups: Add a mirrored Clasp persistence task once the dogfood app has a compiler-managed SQLite-backed counterpart.
Factor the repeated temporary-database helpers out of the app and benchmark tests if more SQLite-backed benchmark slices are added.
Consider reducing or redirecting dirty-tree warning noise in benchmark verification output.
warnings: `ts-lead-persistence` intentionally omits `src/server/store.ts`; task-prep now asserts that absence.
Copy `runtime-modules.d.ts` along with `main.ts` and `store.ts` when proving the canonical persisted solution surface, or TypeScript compilation will fail.
The schema failure is exercised at `createServer(...)` time, before any HTTP request is served.
tests_run: nix develop -c bash -lc 'cd examples/lead-app-ts && npm test'
residual_risks: The new persistence-bearing benchmark is TypeScript-only, so it does not yet participate in a mirrored Clasp-vs-TypeScript comparison.
The benchmark solution snapshot duplicates canonical `examples/lead-app-ts` server files and will need to stay aligned with future app-side SQLite changes.
